title: Lighthouse Project
text:
Lighthouse Project - SupportGoWhere
Lighthouse Project
Share link
Collaboration between Limitless and KKH to provide effective and timely care for youth with mental health conditions who require higher levels of care.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Centre-based
Free
Referral required
SERVICE PROVIDERS
Find service providers near you
Collapse all sections
About this support

The Lighthouse Project aims to be a beacon of light that attracts and correctly directs youths (aged 12-25 years old) with complex moderate to high mental health needs to accessible and appropriate help by doing the following: Providing higher-tiered support at the community level. Senior and highly skilled practitioners at Limitless provide support to youths who would otherwise never get appropriate treatment for complex mental health issues. Right-siting youths requiring higher tiered care by using the therapeutic relationship built with practitioners at Limitless to "cushion" upwards referral into primary, secondary, and tertiary care. Improving standards of care at the community level through preceptorship by senior practitioners nominated by Department of Psychological Medicine (DPM, KKH), and reduction of secondary traumatic stress in the sector through supervision and training of partners by Limitless’ psychologists.

Who is this for?

Youths aged 12-25 with moderate to high mental health needs.

What to expect?

Clients would need to be referred to the programme. Lighthouse project staff would then make contact with the youth for assessment and basic emotional support. Youths may then be referred further. The duration and frequency of sessions would depend on clinician assessment.

How to apply?

Clients need to be referred to the programme by service providers or restructured hospitals.

Information last updated on 29 Sep 2025
